channel.title: Cadence 步频 — PT Research Signal
channel.description: Daily curated physical therapy & rehab research for clinicians. AI-scored signal from 50 sources.

item.title: The effect of exercise on clinical pain: a systematic umbrella review and meta-meta-analysis
item.description: An umbrella review and meta-meta-analysis synthesized evidence from 11 databases, confirming the consistent and significant analgesic effects of exercise across various pain conditions and exercise modalities.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Distinct Patterns of Mobility Recovery After Stroke Using Routine Clinical Data
item.description: A retrospective cohort study of adults admitted for stroke identified distinct subgroups with different mobility recovery trajectories during the first 180 days post-stroke using growth mixture modeling on AM-PAC Basic Mobility scores from routine clinical care.
item.author: medRxiv

item.title: Do blood flow restriction exercises offer additional benefits when compared to conventional exercises in musculoskeletal rehabilitation? A systematic review and meta-analysis.
item.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis found that low-load exercise with blood flow restriction (LLE-BFR) led to greater improvements in muscle strength (SMD 0.51, 95% CI 0.28 to 0.74) and reduced pain (SMD -0.42, 95% CI -0.73 to -0.11) compared to low-load exercise (LLE) alone in musculoskeletal conditions. LLE-BFR was non-inferior to high-load exercise (HLE) in improving muscle strength.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Frontline agreement in diagnosis and treatment for musculoskeletal dysfunctions. A prospective, randomized, controlled study.
item.description: This randomized controlled trial in a Swiss emergency department found good agreement between physiotherapists and resident physicians, compared to senior physicians, in diagnosis, treatment, and discharge decisions for non-urgent musculoskeletal conditions.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Effects of physical exercise on spasticity in people with multiple sclerosis: A systematic review.
item.description: A systematic review of randomized controlled trials concluded that physical exercise interventions may positively influence spasticity in people with multiple sclerosis (EDSS ≤6.0), though the specific effects of different exercise types remain unclear and were narratively synthesized.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Quantifying Physical Activity Through Step Count Estimation Across Multiple Wearable Sensor Configurations: A Validation Study in Post-Stroke and Healthy Adults.
item.description: A validation study involving 18 post-stroke and 18 healthy adults found that different wearable sensor configurations and processing algorithms varied in accuracy for stride count estimation compared to a gold-standard instrumented treadmill across multiple walking speeds and sensor locations (wrists, hips, ankles).
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Development of a Core Patient-Reported Outcome (Measures) Set for Pediatric Physical Therapy.
item.description: This study developed a core set of nine patient-reported outcomes (PROs) and corresponding measures (PROMs) for pediatric physical therapy, grouped into three domains: functioning (physical function, mobility, self-care, learning/applying knowledge, general tasks/demands), life impact (pain, emotional function, social function), and health-related quality of life. The PROs were identified through focus groups, interviews, and a scoping review, then prioritized by pediatric physical therapists.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: The Impact of Mandatory Bundled Payment on Prehabilitation Delivery for Lower Extremity Joint Replacement.
item.description: A difference-in-differences analysis found that the implementation of the Comprehensive Care for Joint Replacement (CJR) mandatory bundled payment model in the US was associated with a 10.3 percentage point reduction in the proportion of hospitals delivering prehabilitation for lower extremity joint replacement, decreasing from 48.7% to 38.4%.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Follow up to the 2018 IOC consensus statement on paediatric ACL injuries: towards a broader vision of care
item.description: This editorial follows up on the 2018 IOC consensus statement on pediatric ACL injuries, emphasizing their emergence as a significant public health concern. It reviews recent advances, current evidence, and persistent challenges in prevention, individualized management, evolving surgical strategies, and long-term holistic care.
item.author: BJSM

item.title: Multimodal Integration of Gait, Balance, and Infrared Thermography Enhances Machine-Learning Classification of Knee Osteoarthritis: A Cross-Sectional Study
item.description: A cross-sectional study investigated whether combining gait, balance, and infrared thermography data improves machine-learning classification of knee osteoarthritis (KOA) versus healthy controls (HC), compared to single-domain models.
item.author: Archives of PM&R

item.title: The effectiveness of cardiovascular rehabilitation for transient ischaemic attack and mild stroke: A randomised controlled trial.
item.description: A randomized controlled trial of 140 TIA and mild stroke participants found that a 6-week cardiovascular rehabilitation program significantly improved functional exercise capacity, physical activity, anthropometrics, blood pressure, and quality-of-life over 6 months compared with usual care.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Prevalence of eating disorders and disordered eating in athletes: a systematic review and meta-analysis
item.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis estimated the prevalence of eating disorders and disordered eating among athletes across training levels and assessment methods, identifying moderators associated with prevalence estimates by searching PubMed/MEDLINE, Scopus, Web of Science, and SportDiscus up to December 2024.
item.author: BJSM

item.title: Patient Engagement, Clinical Outcomes, and Satisfaction of an Advanced Orthopedic Virtual Physical Therapy Model: A Retrospective Cohort Study.
item.description: A retrospective cohort study found that an advanced orthopedic virtual physical therapy model achieved high patient engagement and satisfaction, with 70.9% of referred patients completing full care episodes (exceeding national benchmarks of 43%) and 96% achieving Minimal Clinically Important Differences.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Pelvic floor muscle training for pelvic organ prolapse: A systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials
item.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials confirmed the efficacy of pelvic floor muscle training for pelvic organ prolapse, demonstrating significant improvements in prolapse symptoms and quality of life.
item.author: Sports Medicine

item.title: APTA Report Finds Demand for Pelvic Health Physical Therapy Outpaces Access
item.description: A new APTA report, "APTA State of Pelvic Health Physical Therapy," reveals that the demand for pelvic health physical therapy in the U.S. significantly outpaces access, highlighting workforce, access, and awareness challenges.
item.author: APTA

item.title: Effectiveness of non-pharmacological interventions for lower limb motor impairment in post-stroke hemiplegia: a systematic review and Bayesian network meta-analysis | Journal of NeuroEngineering and Rehabilitation
item.description: A systematic review and Bayesian network meta-analysis evaluated the effectiveness of non-pharmacological interventions for lower limb motor impairment in post-stroke hemiplegia, identifying key effective modalities.
item.author: Sports Medicine

item.title: Pioneering programme launched to test breakthrough spinal treatment in UK rehab centres
item.description: A pioneering program has launched in UK rehab centers to test the real-world impact of Onward Medical's ARC-EX system, a UK-approved spinal cord injury treatment combining targeted electrical spinal cord stimulation with rehabilitation exercises.
item.author: Spinal Surgery News

item.title: Identifying Gaps in Rehabilitation Strategies for Chronic Stroke: A Systematic Review of Evidence in Patients With and Without Nerve Transfer.
item.description: A systematic review identified gaps in rehabilitation strategies for chronic stroke survivors with upper limb impairment, particularly regarding direct comparisons and detailed protocols between contralateral C7 nerve transfer and technology-assisted/high-dose rehabilitation approaches.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Subtype-specific optimal cut-off values of the Berg Balance Scale for predicting independent walking in inpatient stroke rehabilitation: a multicentre cohort study.
item.description: A multicenter retrospective cohort study of 565 stroke patients (316 ischemic, 249 hemorrhagic) in Korea determined subtype-specific optimal cut-off values of the Berg Balance Scale for predicting independent walking at discharge after inpatient rehabilitation.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Sarcopenia Increases Adjacent Segment Degeneration Risk within 3 Years of Anterior Cervical Discectomy and Fusion.
item.description: An exploratory retrospective cohort study of 333 adult patients who underwent elective anterior cervical discectomy and fusion (ACDF) from 2012-2022 found that sarcopenia increases the risk of radiographic adjacent segment degeneration development within 3 years.
item.author: PubMed
